sources.name: MEXT Elementary School Course of Study, Japanese Language
sources.use: Primary native-child elementary Japanese alignment.
sources.licenseStatus: MEXT website terms cover mext.go.jp, permit commercial reuse with attribution, are based on Government Standard Terms of Use 2.0, and are CC BY 4.0 compatible, subject to third-party rights and alternate-term exceptions.
sources.notes: Current dataset stores independently authored topic text and alignment keys only; no Course of Study prose is included.

sources.name: MEXT Lower Secondary School Course of Study, Japanese Language
sources.use: Native lower-secondary alignment mapped to grades 7-9.
sources.licenseStatus: MEXT website terms cover mext.go.jp, permit commercial reuse with attribution, are based on Government Standard Terms of Use 2.0, and are CC BY 4.0 compatible, subject to third-party rights and alternate-term exceptions.
sources.notes: Current dataset stores independently authored topic text and alignment keys only; no Course of Study prose is included.

sources.name: MEXT High School Course of Study, Japanese Language
sources.use: Native high-school Japanese alignment mapped to grades 10-12.
sources.licenseStatus: MEXT website terms cover mext.go.jp, permit commercial reuse with attribution, are based on Government Standard Terms of Use 2.0, and are CC BY 4.0 compatible, subject to third-party rights and alternate-term exceptions.
sources.notes: Current dataset stores independently authored topic text and alignment keys only; no Course of Study prose is included.

sources.name: Japanese Course of Study Linked Open Data
sources.use: Stable curriculum codes and metadata.
sources.licenseStatus: Course of Study LOD states that the structured RDF/Turtle dataset is published under the same license as the original code table, CC BY 4.0.
sources.notes: Used for stable curriculum codes, URIs, and metadata rather than explanatory prose.

sources.name: MEXT grade-wise kanji allocation table
sources.use: Kyoiku kanji grade tags.
sources.licenseStatus: MEXT website terms cover mext.go.jp, permit commercial reuse with attribution, are based on Government Standard Terms of Use 2.0, and are CC BY 4.0 compatible, subject to third-party rights and alternate-term exceptions.
sources.notes: Current companion data uses grade-level kanji metadata only; individual kanji are not graph nodes and table prose is not included.

sources.name: Agency for Cultural Affairs Joyo kanji table
sources.use: Joyo kanji and okurigana alignment.
sources.licenseStatus: Agency for Cultural Affairs site policy points users to the MEXT website terms; those terms cover bunka.go.jp, permit commercial reuse with attribution, and are CC BY 4.0 compatible, subject to third-party rights and alternate-term exceptions.
sources.notes: Used as a literacy alignment signal; current seed does not copy the full Joyo table or explanatory prose.

sources.name: Yookoso! An Invitation to Contemporary Japanese
sources.use: Adult L2 beginner sequence and coverage reference.
sources.licenseStatus: Copyrighted textbook; do not copy prose, dialogues, exercises, examples, or page text.
sources.notes: Used only for high-level topic alignment and independently authored graph nodes.

sources.name: Yookoso! Continuing with Contemporary Japanese
sources.use: Adult L2 intermediate sequence and coverage reference.
sources.licenseStatus: Copyrighted textbook; do not copy prose, dialogues, exercises, examples, or page text.
sources.notes: Used only for high-level topic alignment and independently authored graph nodes.

sources.name: Tae Kim's Guide to Learning Japanese
sources.use: Grammar coverage and sequencing check.
sources.licenseStatus: Licensing differs by version; use as reference only unless permission is verified.
sources.notes: Dataset does not copy guide explanations or examples.

sources.name: JGram / JGram-derived grammar indexes
sources.use: Grammar-point inventory and JLPT-level cross-check.
sources.licenseStatus: License unclear; use only as a coverage checklist until reviewed.
sources.notes: Dataset does not copy grammar explanations or examples.

sources.name: JF Standard for Japanese-Language Education
sources.use: CEFR/JF proficiency alignment and can-do framing.
sources.licenseStatus: Current seed uses JF/CEFR level tags and independently authored task framing only; full JF Can-do text, guidebook prose, site images, or downloadable materials require separate Japan Foundation terms review.
sources.notes: Dataset stores level tags and independently authored task descriptions; it does not copy JF Can-do statements.

sources.name: Japanese-Language Proficiency Test
sources.use: General-language proficiency tags from N5 through N1.
sources.licenseStatus: Use level metadata only; do not copy exam content.
sources.notes: JLPT tags are alignment metadata, not a full test-content import.

sources.name: BJT Business Japanese Proficiency Test
sources.use: Business/professional Japanese alignment through J1+.
sources.licenseStatus: Use official level metadata only; do not copy sample questions or test content.
sources.notes: BJT tags identify workplace/professional task alignment inside the adult L2 graph.

sources.name: Iwasaki (2006), The Acquisition of Japanese as a Second Language and Processability Theory
sources.use: Phenomenon-level citation for a longitudinal child JSL case examining the emergence of verbal inflection, V-te V, passive, and causative structures.
sources.licenseStatus: Bibliographic metadata and original taxonomy alignment notes only; no thesis prose, tables, examples, or assessment material included.
sources.notes: Single-case naturalistic child study. A mapping identifies a studied linguistic phenomenon only; it does not establish this adult taxonomy's JLPT placement, prerequisites, teaching sequence, or mastery threshold.

sources.name: Iwasaki (2013), A Processability Theory-based Analysis of Japanese Morphology and Syntax
sources.use: Phenomenon-level citation for an intensive adult JSL case examining emergence across verbal affixes, V-te V, passive, causative, benefactive, and sentence structures.
sources.licenseStatus: Bibliographic metadata and original taxonomy alignment notes only; no article prose, tables, examples, or assessment material included.
sources.notes: Single-case study of one intensive adult learner. A mapping identifies a studied linguistic phenomenon only; it does not validate a general adult acquisition order, JLPT placement, teaching sequence, or mastery threshold.

sources.name: Iwasaki and Oliver (2014), The Acquisition of Verbal Morphology in JSL by a Child: Emergence and Accuracy
sources.use: Citation for the distinction between first emergence and target-like accuracy in one longitudinal child JSL corpus.
sources.licenseStatus: Bibliographic metadata and original taxonomy alignment notes only; no article prose, tables, examples, or assessment material included.
sources.notes: Single-case naturalistic child study. The taxonomy's learning-log routine is a processability-informed pedagogical application authored by this project, not an intervention or diagnostic validated by the publication.

sources.name: Iwasaki and Oliver (2018), Describing the Acquisition of the Passive Voice by a Child Learner of JSL
sources.use: Phenomenon-level citation for passive-voice development in one longitudinal child JSL case from a Processability Theory perspective.
sources.licenseStatus: Bibliographic metadata and original taxonomy alignment notes only; no article prose, tables, examples, or assessment material included.
sources.notes: Single-case naturalistic child study. A mapping identifies passive voice as the studied phenomenon only; it does not establish this adult taxonomy's N3 placement, prerequisites, teaching sequence, or mastery threshold.

sources.name: Kawaguchi and Iwasaki (2021), Acquiring Content Questions in Japanese
sources.use: Phenomenon-level citation for content-question development in one sequential English-Japanese bilingual child.
sources.licenseStatus: Bibliographic metadata and original taxonomy alignment notes only; no article prose, tables, examples, or assessment material included.
sources.notes: Single-child study. A mapping identifies Japanese content questions as the studied phenomenon only; it does not establish this adult taxonomy's A1 or JLPT N5 placement, prerequisites, teaching sequence, or mastery threshold.

sources.name: Project-authored native curriculum spiral
sources.use: Identifies project-authored native-child spiral topics spanning recurring skills across grades.
sources.licenseStatus: Project-authored taxonomy text released under the repository MIT License.
sources.notes: Used to separate structural coverage from educator-approved release quality; not an external source.

sources.name: Project-authored adult L2 curriculum spiral
sources.use: Identifies project-authored adult L2 spiral topics spanning recurring skills across proficiency levels.
sources.licenseStatus: Project-authored taxonomy text released under the repository MIT License.
sources.notes: Used to separate structural coverage from teacher-approved release quality; not an external source.
